name: Jill Gostin
abstract:
Jill I. Gostin is a Principal Research Scientist Emeritus at the Georgia Tech Research Institute (GTRI), where her work focused on sensor systems and software. She also served as the systems engineering, integration, and test lead in the software engineering and architecture division. Her publications, service and technical awards, large program management, and leadership of groups including industry, government, and academia have contributed to her career success. 

She has served IEEE extensively, including three years on the IEEE Board of Directors, as 2023 IEEE Vice President of Member and Geographic Activities and 2020-2021 Region 3 Director. She is a former chair of the IEEE Atlanta Section and the IEEE Computer Society’s Atlanta Chapter. Gostin served on the IEEE Computer and IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems societies’ boards of governors and was the vice president of finance for the Sensor Council’s Executive Committee. She has also led or been a member of several IEEE organizational units and committees, locally and globally.

Her leadership has been recognized by being named 2016 Woman of the Year by Georgia Women in Technology and receiving the 2025 IEEE Women in Technology & Leadership Award.

name: Anuradha C Bahl
title: Amplifying Human Potential for an AI-Powered Future
abstract:
Anu Bahl is responsible for directing multiple transformational
initiatives focused on improving design timeline efficiencies and
driving software tool/license standardization to enhance affordability and delivery speed, as well
as strengthen mission performance.

Throughout her career, she has held several pivotal leadership roles spanning cross-functional
team leadership, business strategy, and program execution across signal processing, advanced
spacecraft bus systems, payload integration and RF sensor electronics. She has a proven track
record of translating complex technical challenges into scalable business and operational
outcomes.

She has completed several leadership development programs over the course of her career and
has been recognized with multiple awards, including “Rising Star,” “Employee of the Year,”
“Move the Needle” and “Operational Excellence,” reflecting her significant contributions to
program execution and enterprise-wide operational transformation.

She holds a bachelor’s degree in electronics and telecommunication from India and a master’s
degree in electrical and computer engineering from Utah State University. Outside of work, she
enjoys traveling with her family, baking, reading, and expressing her creativity through her
passion for art.

name: Stephanie Gillespie
affiliation: Associate Dean, Tagliatela College of Engineering at the University of New Haven | Chair, IEEE Humanitarian Technologies Education Committee
title: Get connected: How HTB can help you make an impact
abstract:
The IEEE Humanitarian Technologies Board (HTB) inspires and empowers IEEE volunteers around the world carrying out and supporting impactful humanitarian technology activities at the local level.

Learn more about the HTB programs and opportunities, and then dive into an interactive session where you will identify local challenges in your community that you can help solve.

Learn best tips on connecting with communities, and start to prepare your pitch for later in the workshop

name: Russell Harrison
title: Constraining AI through the Political Process
abstract: Russell Harrison serves as Managing Director of IEEE-USA, the American component of the Institute of Electrical and Electronic Engineers, and Director of Global Policy for IEEE. Russell has over 20 years of experience working in Washington, D.C. representing America's technology community. His global team of policy experts works to help governments enact technically sound public policies while encouraging innovation and societal improvement. Beyond public policy, IEEE-USA specializes in innovative collaborations within America's technology community, especially bringing innovators in smaller communities together to maximize their innovative capacity.

name: Shankar Krishnan
title: How are AI agents creating new opportunities for your professionals across various industries?
abstract: I bring 15+ years of AI/ML experience, with the last five years focused specifically on Generative AI at AWS, where I led product development within the Amazon Bedrock GenAI organization for conversational AI. In that role, I launched and scaled multiple 0-to-1 ML products. Prior to AWS, I led conversational AI initiatives for IBM Watson serving large enterprise clients, and held AI product leadership roles at Aptiv (autonomous vehicles) and Upserve (AI analytics for SMBs).

name: Phane Mane
affiliation: Global Lead - AI Product Strategy/Delivery, Boston Scientific
title: The AI Career Playbook: Where to Start, What to Learn, and How to Grow
abstract:
AI is reshaping every industry and every career path. In this session, Phane Mane, an OMBA Cohort 2 alum, shares practical frameworks for developing an AI-ready mindset, aligning business education with emerging AI-driven roles, and future-proofing careers through lifelong learning and experimentation. Drawing on real-world leadership experience, students will gain actionable strategies for leveraging AI tools, building differentiated skills, and positioning themselves as leaders in an AI-powered business world.

Phane led AI product delivery at Boston Scientific and is based in the Boston area. He can be reached at https://www.linkedin.com/in/phanemane/

name: Aravindhan Anbazhagan
affiliation: Board of Governors, IEEE Professional Communication Society
title: Staying Relevant in the Age of AI: The Human Skills That Still Matter
abstract: Aravindhan Anbazhagan is a graduate student in Public Policy and Management at Carnegie Mellon University and an internationally recognized IEEE volunteer leader with over ten years of service across student, young professional, and professional communities. He serves on the Board of Governors of the IEEE Professional Communication Society and is a recipient of the IEEE Richard E. Merwin Student Scholarship, Larry K. Wilson Outstanding Asia-Pacific Student Volunteer Award, and IEEE MGA Young Professionals Achievement Award.

name: Irene Ko
title: Looking Inside AI Language Models: Inspecting and Steering AI as It Runs
abstract: Dr. Ching-Yun (Irene) Ko is a Staff Research Scientist at IBM Research. Her research focuses primarily on trustworthy machine learning. Her research focuses on trustworthy machine learning and large language models, particularly model safety, alignment, and robustness across training-time and runtime interventions. Dr. Ko has published papers in esteemed AI/ML conferences, including ICML, NeurIPS, ICLR, IROS, IJCAI, and AAAI, where she has given talks and often served as a program committee member or a reviewer. Her research also extends to efficient scientific computing, in which she has authored papers in renowned journals and conferences such as Automatica, IEEE Transactions on Image Processing, and IEEE Conference on Decision and Control. Dr. Ko received her Ph.D. in electrical engineering and computer science from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Cambridge, USA, in 2024.

name: Abir Chermiti
title: IEEE Industry AI Labs: Connecting Young Professionals to AI Skills, Tools, and Industry Opportunities
abstract:
Abir Chermiti is a strategic industry engagement leader at IEEE, advancing global initiatives at the intersection of artificial intelligence, industry collaboration, workforce transformation, and professional development. She is deeply engaged in IEEE's AI-focused efforts, including initiatives that explore how AI can support career development, applied learning, industry engagement, and responsible innovation. Her work includes helping shape programs such as IEEE Industry AI Labs and AI-enabled career tools that connect IEEE members, young professionals, students, volunteers, and industry leaders with practical opportunities to learn, build, and collaborate around emerging technologies.

Abir is passionate about making AI more accessible, useful, and relevant for professionals navigating the future of work. She brings strong enthusiasm for human-centered AI, cross-disciplinary collaboration, and the role IEEE can play in helping technical communities turn AI innovation into real-world impact.

name: Kartik Srinivasan
title: AI for Autonomous Vehicles: From Foundation Models to Safety Assurance
abstract:
Kartik Srinivasan is a Staff Systems Engineer specializing in Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) Verification & Validation (V&V) for autonomous vehicles. With over a decade of experience across automotive and autonomous mobility systems, he has played a pivotal role in shaping HIL V&V strategies for Level-4 autonomous trucks at Torc Robotics.

His expertise spans HIL architecture design, system integration, closed-loop simulation, and test automation for safety-critical vehicle functions. Previously, Kartik led HIL validation initiatives at Motional, where he contributed to the development of Drive-by-Wire and Perception subsystem testing frameworks. His earlier work at Nexteer Automotive and LG Chemical Power strengthened his foundation in mechatronic test benches, functional safety engineering, and battery management systems. Kartik holds a Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering from Michigan Technological University. He is passionate about AV safety, AI-assisted validation, and scalable test frameworks, and has actively contributed to the advancement of autonomous mobility with 4 U.S. patents filed (Patent Pending).

He is an IEEE Senior Member and, in Dec 2025, was inducted as a Professional Member of IEEE Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) into the Eta Chapter of the Board of Governors, recognizing his sustained technical contributions and leadership in the field.

name: Sam Stone
affiliation: AI/ML Engineer at Lockheed Martin | IEEE Region 1 Young Professionals Chair
title: IEEE Regions 1+2 Young Professionals
abstract: Sam Stone is an Artificial Intelligence Engineer at Lockheed Martin, specializing in radar signal processing. Sam has a BS in Electrical Engineering from Binghamton University and has several publications related to signal processing, autonomous vehicles, deep learning, large language models, and radar. Sam has been an active member of the IEEE for 10 years and is currently the IEEE Region 1 Young Professionals Chair and the IEEE Syracuse Section Treasurer.

name: Juniyali Nauriyal
title: Building a Deep-Tech Startup in an Era of Photonic Acceleration Due to AI
abstract: Juniyali Nauriyal is the CEO and Co-Founder of Photonect. She holds a master’s degree in optics and a PhD from the University of Rochester’s Institute of Optics. Juniyali is the co-inventor of laser-assisted fiber-attach technology, which she is now commercializing through Photonect. As CEO, she leads a growing startup at the intersection of advanced photonic packaging and real-world industrial deployment. Her work has earned recognition through highly competitive programs, including the Activate Fellowship and the Luminate Accelerator. In 2025, she was named Emerging Technology Woman of the Year. Juniyali serves as Vice Chair of IEEE WIE Rochester, where she actively supports initiatives that empower women in engineering.

name: Madhushree Kumari
affiliation: Staff Software Engineer at Visa | Chair, IEEE Young Professionals Atlanta
title: Architecting the Real-Time Pipelines that Power AI in Financial Markets
abstract:
Madhushree Kumari is a Staff Software Engineer at Visa with over 13 years of experience architecting scalable distributed systems. She currently leads a global team within Visa’s FX trading platform, specializing in resilient microservices and cloud-native solutions. Her career spans impactful engineering roles at SAP, McAfee, IBM, and FIS.

An alumna of Georgia Tech and currently pursuing AI studies at Stanford, Madhushree is a recognized leader in the tech community. She serves as Chair for IEEE Young Professionals Atlanta, Secretary for the IEEE Atlanta Section, and is a Founding Member of WomenTech Network. Her contributions earned her the 2025 WomenTech Global Silver Award for Software Engineering Leader of the Year. Beyond engineering, she is a frequent keynote speaker, peer reviewer, and mentor for organizations including AnitaB.org and Georgia Tech.

name: Yuhan (Sicily) Zheng
title: A Guidebook on AI’s Applications in the Energy Transition
abstract:
Yuhan (Sicily) Zheng, PhD student in Geography at the National University of Ireland.

Currently works as the Executive Assistant to the CEO, Nobel Sustainability Trust (NST).

She is the chair at the IEEE Young Professional Climate and Sustainability subcommittee. She is co-chairing the United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE) Youth Group on Resilient Energy Systems (YGRES).

name: Giselle Zeno
title: Does your AI Really See What You Think It Sees?
abstract:
Dr. Giselle Zeno is a technical staff member in the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Technology and Systems Group at MIT Lincoln Laboratory. She is an IEEE Senior Member and a member of the ACM society. She has published multiple research papers in top-ranked machine learning (ML) conferences and workshops. She has also served as organizing chair and technical committee for workshops on the topics of AI assurance and graph machine learning, and as a reviewer for IEEE journals, ACM conferences, and the Tapia conference. Prior to her career as an AI and ML researcher, she worked in industry as a senior software engineer.

Dr. Zeno earned her BS degree in computer science from the University of Puerto Rico at Bayamon in 2010, graduating Magna Cum Laude. She was named Model Student by the Department of Computer Science and received the Honors Program scholarship throughout her undergraduate studies. She completed her MS and PhD degrees in computer science at Purdue University in 2021 and 2023, respectively. Her doctoral studies were supported by the GEM Fellowship, sponsored by Intel Corporation, and the Frederick N. Andrews Fellowship from Purdue University.

name: Ed Palacio
title: YP Listening Lab: What’s Working, What’s Missing, What’s Next
abstract: Ed Palacio is President of P&L Technical Management Solutions, a small business focused on technology, program management, and business development for the military and civilian market. As a business executive, he has over 40 years of experience directing the development and production of complex military electronic systems. He has held Profit and Loss responsibility at the Business Unit and Sector levels, as well as functional lead positions in Program Management, Engineering, and Operations. Prior to his current position, he was VP of Program Management and Business Operations for ITT/Exelis Electronic Systems. Before joining ITT, Ed was VP of EDO Corporation’s Electronic Warfare Sector. He is or has been a member of multiple advisory boards in industry, academia, and professional organizations. He currently serves on the Board of Directors of Werlatone Corporation. He was the 2023 IEEE-USA President and the 2020-2021 director of IEEE’s Northeast Region, has sat on multiple Executive Boards for IEEE, and has served on Industry Advisory Boards for The Cooper Union School of Engineering, SUNY Stony Brook Engineering Department, and the New York Institute of Technology College of Engineering and Computing Sciences Dean Executive Advisory Board. He teaches at Stony Brook University’s Department of Technology and Society. Ed holds a BEE from the Cooper Union School of Engineering and Science and two MS degrees in Electrical Engineering and Technology Management from the Polytechnic Institute of New York.

name: Michele Shindelman
affiliation: Vice President, Global Human Resources at MKS Inc.
title: WIE Panel: Driving Technology & Leadership Management in the face of Transformative Disruption
abstract:
Ms. Shindelman serves as Vice President, Global Human Resources of MKS Inc. Ms. Shindelman joined MKS in 2016 and has held positions of increasing responsibility within the Human Resources organization. Most recently, she has served as Vice President, Global Human Resources, following roles as Senior Director of Human Resources and Human Resources Director.

Prior to joining MKS, Ms. Shindelman held Human Resources leadership positions at E Ink Corporation, Symmetricom, American Superconductor Corporation, and the Keystone Group, supporting global organizations across the energy, telecommunications, materials, and financial services industries. Throughout her career, she has partnered closely with executive and management teams to develop and implement Human Resources strategies that align with business objectives, support growth, and enable organizational transformation.

Ms. Shindelman holds a B.A. in Economics from Brandeis University and an M.B.A. in Management from Boston College.

name: Varshitha Manjunath
affiliation: Assistant Professor and Cybersecurity Program Lead at Lasell University
title: WIE Panel: Driving Technology & Leadership Management in the face of Transformative Disruption
abstract: Varshitha Manjunath is an Assistant Professor and Cybersecurity Program Lead at Lasell University, Secretary of the IEEE Boston Section, Faculty Advisor for Women in CyberSecurity (WiCyS) Lasell Chapter, and Co-Director of Tech Walk Boston. She plays an active role in leading professional and technical communities, contributing to the advancement of cybersecurity and emerging technologies.

name: Vasundhara (Vasu) Bhupathi
affiliation: Strategic Account Leader & SAP Business AI Leader - Americas, DXC Technology | IEEE Senior Member
title: WIE Panel: Driving Technology & Leadership Management in the face of Transformative Disruption
abstract:
Vasu Bhupathi leads at the intersection of enterprise technology, governance, and people, exactly where transformative disruption is felt hardest. Over 23 years, she has translated AI strategy into operational reality: architected and sponsored development of an Agentic AI solution for a global pharmaceutical client; served as a Strategic Delivery leader sustaining 59+ consecutive months of 100% SLA compliance across a multimillion-dollar Aerospace & Defense portfolio; and led teams of 60+ through modernization, cloud migration, and Scaled Agile adoption.

Her leadership extends beyond the enterprise. As Chairperson of the IEEE Connecticut Women in Engineering Executive Committee and a co-author of four peer-reviewed publications, she brings research rigor to workforce conversations. As a FIRST Robotics coach and judge evaluating 50+ student teams annually, she works daily with the next generation preparing them to enter the workforce, giving her a rare, ground-level view of what intergenerational leadership actually requires.

Vasu doesn’t theorize about disruption. She builds the systems, mentors the people, and sets the governance that determines who thrives through it.

name: Vidya Wadkar
title: Responsible Agentic AI: Designing Human-Centered Autonomy for the Future of Work
abstract: Vidya Wadkar is a Distinguished Engineer at Verizon with more than 18 years of experience in enterprise technology, AI systems architecture, cloud-native modernization, intelligent automation, and large-scale digital transformation. Her work focuses on designing practical, scalable AI solutions that augment professionals, improve operational efficiency, and strengthen enterprise decision-making. She has led and contributed to initiatives involving conversational AI, agentic AI frameworks, observability-driven intelligence, secure platform modernization, and automation for complex business environments.

name: Christine Miyachi
affiliation: Principal Software Engineering Manager at Microsoft | Chair, IEEE AI Coalition
title: AI and the Future of Software Development
abstract: Christine Miyachi has almost 30 years of experience working for startups and large corporations. Currently, she is a Principal Software Engineering Manager in the Cloud+AI division at Microsoft, where she works in the Health and Life Sciences division. She holds two MIT degrees: an MS in technology and policy/electrical engineering and computer science and an MS in System Design and Management. See more about Chris at www.christinemiyachi.com. Chris was the past Chair of IEEE Future Directions and currently leads the IEEE AI Coalition. Fun fact: She has run 30+ marathons and just got her eighth world marathon star medal in South Africa this year. https://www.linkedin.com/in/chris-miyachi-a31835

name: Ahsan Qamer Sheikh
affiliation: Planning Engineer for Rail Infrastructure at Ramboll Deutschland GmbH | Chair, IEEE Young Professionals Germany
title: YP Panel
abstract:
Ahsan Qamer Sheikh is a Planning Engineer for Rail Infrastructure at Ramboll Deutschland GmbH in Munich, Germany, where he works on the planning and design of large-scale rail infrastructure and transportation projects across Germany. He holds a master’s degree in Infrastructure Planning from the University of Stuttgart, specializing in transportation systems and infrastructure development.

Ahsan has been an active IEEE volunteer for more than 12 years. He currently serves as Chair of IEEE Young Professionals Germany, where he leads initiatives that promote professional development, networking, and collaboration among early-career engineers and technology professionals. He also serves as the Young Professional Representative for the IEEE Professional Communication Society and as an Initiative Lead at IEEE MGA SAC.

In recognition of his contributions to IEEE and the engineering community, Ahsan received the 2025 IEEE MGA Young Professional Achievement Award and the 2025 IEEE Young Professionals Hall of Fame Award for sustainable contributions and impact.

Ahsan is committed to strengthening collaboration between industry and the IEEE community, with a particular focus on creating opportunities for students and Young Professionals. His initiatives aim to foster professional networking, mentorship, industry engagement, and knowledge exchange, helping early-career engineers develop the skills and connections needed to thrive.
